channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 136 replies — 236.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 136 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:01 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283715
>>62283702
>without having to interact with Hedera at all
Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1"), or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

62284227
>>62283896
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62284232
>>62284227
>1pbtid
keep swapping

62284314
>>62283922
It seems like nobody here really knows anything about CLPR. Hell they didn't even know it existed two days ago lol.

62284575
>>62278842
Wow another private key hacked. Chainlink would have prevented this.

62284723
>>62284232
lick my ass, nigger

62284748
>>62284723
>lick my ass, nigger

62285049
>>62283946
>theres no sauce
I get it, this is a curveball, but if we lie to ourselves, like saying Chainlink is the only solution forever and ever, then we sound like xrpcels. the only thing posted as been press releases, nothing more, otherwise i wouldve read that instead of talking to low iq biz retards.
>>62284314
ikr. it’s been almost a month, wish there was more than silence. cll governs hbar they know how clpr works, wish theyd share.
>>62283951
Think what you like faggot. again, suck these hairy nuts and enjoy.

62285504
>>62285049
>>theres no sauce
You were spoonfed the sauce as a url in a direct reply. What is wrong with you.

62285523
>>62285049
>if we lie to ourselves
>we
fuck off

62285569
i'm too demoralized to read this thread. just tell me, are we going to make it?

62285584
>>62285569
CLPR just buckbroke CCIP.
But the rest of Chainlink services are fine.

62285645
>>62285584
Nobody will ever fall for the HBAR shilling, you are so fucking bad at this.

62285660
>>62285645
CLPR doesn't even benefit HBAR
Anyone can use it without using hedera

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 36 replies — 144.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:33 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285071
>>62285047
Bears are getting a little too cocky

62285103
>>62285047
IMy bad, it's not lagging, but usually these indicators fail at big movements like what we are seeing, if you followed and bought btc at 78k you would have to sell at a loss at 77 and derisk at 76k.

Kronos try the same but aligning with Monte Carlo simulaations and guess what? it's been missing a lot of directions and turns in the market.https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/

If this indicator is any good, just post how it portrayed the dump in January (I bet it didn't do well).

These indicators only work until they don't and work better when the market is sideways.

62285118
>>62285071
It's like they don't even see the green arrow for tomorrow

>>62285103
>If this indicator is any good, just post how it portrayed the dump in January (I bet it didn't do well).
This one? Looks like if you sold/short red and bought green you made a ton of money

62285172
>>62285118
Interesting

62285202
>>62285172
Is this saylors indicator?

62285204
>>62285172
>feb 8, 2026: model picks out mar 3 as a high-probability buy date
>price spikes that day
also
>first week of a war
>ha ha on feb 8 you didn't predict that!
the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year, on dates given weeks in advance. the twitter account's been posting since october

62285210
>>62285204
High hit rate doesn't mean good returns.

>It still underperforms Buy & Hold

KEK

62285226
>>62285202
what our friend doesn't understand is that the price on that date is the close, so unless you bought at exactly the very last possible second, you made money, especially since the next signal is "derisk" (yellow diamond)

>>62285210
>It still underperforms Buy & Hold
>+963% over Buy & Hold is underperforming Buy & Hold
Do you have literal brain damage

62285271
Lets see ETH

62285277
>>62285271
I only have BTC implemented right now, but next week I'm adding ETH, SOL and DOGE
they'll be similar since they essentially trade as beta plays to the orange coin

62285293
>>62285034
>>62285103
>>62285172
>>62285210
I want to take a moment to thank our special needs investor anon here, what a hero
>that's a lagging indicator!
>well ok it's actually forecasting a month in advance, but i bet it sucked in january!
>ok so it crushed january but what about this one single date!
>ok so it predicted a price spike on that date nearly a month in advance but the demo strategy is only beating buy & hold by 963%, that's underperforming buy & hold!
>ok i just realized +963% over buy & hold means more than 10x buy & hold but...!

62285323
71.8/71.4 will be the institutional floor. This is the bedrock number they're trying to drag it towards before June 1st.
74k would have been the floor had retail not dumped the other weekend. Good job morons.

62285343
>>62284969
What exactly do you mean by derisk?

62285356
explain me how to use this and i'll try it for 1 week and 1 month on hyperliquid (or extended)

62285361
>>62285343
Move into non-volatile assets, usually USD.

62285363
>>62285343
>What exactly do you mean by derisk?
Whatever your trading style/risk tolerance is - you could reduce size, reduce leverage, move stops up etc. The demo 1x lev BTC strategy just closes 50% on confirmation to keep it simple

>>62285356
see
>>62284969
>green = buy dips
>red = sell rips
>yellow = volatility likely, derisk
>the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
>today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
>chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62285389
>>62285363
ok, im reading about it, surely you understand im skeptic, right? 1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
what do you recommend in terms of setting (sl for shorts longs the same?)
if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits

62285426
>>62285389
>1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
>what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
up to your own style. Like I said, the demo strategy just sells 50% on Volatility confirmation, since the goal of that strategy is just to show how an almost-passive investor can make gains with 4-6 trades a month
One user scalps ETH liquidation levels using it as a bull/bear bias and says he's printing
>if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits
no need anon, I'm not charging anyone money until I've got the model tuned into ridiculous accuracy. maybe give the twitter a follow and turn notes on, leave a like or comment on the posts, that'd be a big help

62285441
>>62285363
Ah I see. But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.

Anyways my experience with short term trading crypto is you get eaten alive by the fees. Not only are the fees exorbitantly higher than for stocks but you get charged per dollar not per coin/share. For example on kraken you have to be doing over a million in monthly volume just to get your fees down to a reasonable rate and even thats assuming you never make market orders.

62285468
You guys still use meme indicators? I simply look at the moon

62285477
>>62285441
>But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.
The Volatility signals are weird inflection points where you can get either early trend reversals, or sudden trend accelerations - combining it with the FX layer is really good at identifying which of those it's likely to be, and confirming/invalidating the signal.
For example,
>>62285172
Here you got a Buy followed by a Volatility with FX counter-trend to price trend (negative) - the implication is exactly what happened, a short-term bullish impulse
>>62284814
The May 25 Sell was followed by the 26th Volatility with inline FX to price trend (negative) - implies acceleration to the downside, and man did that happen

62285480
>>62285468
Why look at the moon when you can look at the wave anon

62285577
how do i find this? website?

62285614
>>62285577
Watermark

62285615
>>62285426
>I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
yeah, most likely. Definitely tune it, it's super useful to understand how tight should the strategy be.

explain me something, i'm seeing two future signals (may 29 and may 30), this won't change if i understand correctly, that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website? but the confirmation layer will only be updated in that day, yes? I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
Finally when are the trades done? Immediately when a new daily candle starts? Or is there any rule?

i'm going to give you a follow for sure

62285636
>>62285615
>that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website?
First one that confirms. If FX continues this positive tomorrow, 2h after US open it'll confirm. Honestly I think buys today are good but that's not what the forecast back on the 3rd said
> I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
The FX gauges are the confirmation layer, Strategy is an oscillator using several weighted factors (physics predictions, FX on different timeframes, seasonality, etc) that's supposed to give you a simple, accurate bias for the day. It's all WIP but in its defense it was screaming Strong Sell on the 25th and man was that accurate
>Finally when are the trades done?
They execute immediately on confirmation, right now I'm working on a fully automated bot that just uses Strategy and a basic Bollinger strat for entries/exits
>i'm going to give you a follow for sure
thanks anon! Likes and comments on the posts are maybe the biggest possible help right now

62285706
>>62285636
is there anything i can read about the schizo psychics this is based on?

62285733
>>62285706
From the other thread, Econophysics as a concept has been around since Bacherie's "Theorie de la speculation" in 1900, but what I'm doing is based on "Price variations in a stock market with many agents" from 1997 as a foundation, and "Financial brownian particle in the layered order-book fluid and fluctuation-dissipation relations" from 2014, and then applying a pretty simple schizo concept to take the reasoning one step further. The end result is predictive weeks in advance

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 117 replies — 119.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-05-28 19:37 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

item.title: PIECE OF SHIT — 18 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
PIECE OF SHIT

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:09 UTC

OP

PIECE OF SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62284559
>>62284554
Hedge against inflation btw lol

62284562
>>62284554
we are in a bear market. Just accept it.

Only retarded newfags and desperate baggies keep denying it

62284579
>>62284554
WTFWTFWTF :D

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284604
MOON IN JUNE MY NIGGGGGGAAAAAS

62284623
>>62284554
Interestingly enough it's completely tearing apart non-stablecoin crypto as it's nearing 80% marketcap.

62284630
>>62284554
>Not a store of value
>Doesn't beat the index
>Costs more to move than just using tradfi
Lmao. No fucking use case.

62284636
>>62284554
Bitcoin's primary purpose these past 5 years has been to dump on Link; what did you expect to happen?

62284657
40k October
Simple as

62284667
>>62284554
fucking TURD

62284685
>>62284630
bullish

62284725
wait wait wait

there are "people" holding Bitcoin thinking we are going back to a new ATH before dumping sub50K?

kys seriously

62284750
>>62284657
I'm buying at 30k

62284767
>>62284554
New here? Keep cexxing!

62285369
>>62284657
40k Oct, then crabs for years

62285392
>>62284554
>He's not holding at least 1 btc and at least an equivalent portfolio heavily exposed to S&P
Yikes finances 101 kid

62285455
>>62284725

62285571
>>62284554
Isn't this what you wanted?
An opposite hedge digital gold against markets?

62285693
>62285369
>40k Oct, then
dumps even more!!!

item.title: ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:35 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285243
>>62285128
crazy the amount of time people spend fudding a random alt coin.
it happens so much, the amount of energy that's spent on it buy a group of individuals, or a bot? either way, it's very interesting from the outside
made me start to think the linkies are onto something..
to anyone else on the outside, like anyone here from pmg or smg? how does this look? pretty crazy to me..
anyway the constant fud against link made me think i should secure a suicide bag of it, and now i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
so must be trying to hide something

62285245
quick, make more threads!

62285257
>>62285128
so true, king

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285535
>>62285243
>i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
STFU nigga you aint doin that dont lie fees would rape u

62285592
>>62285535
like 1-2 cent a buy?

62285749
>>62285128
>chainlink staking mechanism colorized

62285799
>>62285128
Ikyk

item.title: SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. — 37 replies — 23.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP.

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-05-28 19:00 UTC

OP

SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. Why aren't you investing in their IPO?

Early replies (first 3)

62273501
>>62273487
If AI ends up being a nothing burger, then SpaceX is little more than a flashy telecommunications company.
Everyone in the world is betting the farm on AI too, what happens if a different company achieves AGI before SpaceX?

62273502
People still don't understand what printing money does to an economy.

62273506
>>62273487
>SpaceX
>Space
the name of the fucking company is 0.1% of its TAM golly gee maybe they should call it AIX?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273615
IDK about space, but I'm bullish on tesla and U.S. stocks. I still think rockets are a scam and they already have UFO's that are capable of long distance space travel.

By 2030 solid state battery electric cars will be here in full force in tesla for consumers, making ev's and they'll be exceptional deals, 300K miles with barely any wear compared to a gas engine car. Incredibly efficient compared to gas cars and last longer.

Agentic AI will be in cars, self driving will be good by then.

62273662
>>62273487
"SpaceX estimates it's own TAM by making up a bunch of bullshit"

62273749
>>62273511
Its not, according to muricans.

62274456
>>62273749
Well, it’s not any bigger than Texas.
That’s why they have to launch the rockets from Texas.

62274498
>>62273487
SpaceX TAM eternally va US GDP In one year.

62274836
>>62274498
TAM is measured in annual revenue

62275036
>>62273487
Maybe if he was mining asteroids for precious metals, but this is ridiculous
Twitter is not worth that much money
Grok isn't worth that much money
This is a scam

62275067
>>62275036
Asteroid mining is the kind of thing that sounds like a good idea, until you think about the practicalities of running mining equipment and refining plants in microgravity, the delta-v requirements of bringing the stuff home, and the likely lack of any easily accessible ices that could be turned into rocket propellant on any asteroid that orbits anywhere near the sun

62275088
>Why aren't you investing in their IPO?
There aren't enough shares for plebs like me to short.
Same reason I sat in the cuck chair as people in my discord made six figures on CBRS shorts. No volume for plebbies.

62275111
I am going to put $500 into SpaceX and then hopefully one day long after I am dead my money will make it to the moons of Saturn

62275148
>>62275111
It will moke likely crash into Uranus after the IPO

62275195
>>62273487
>IPO
Point me to an IPO that wasn't a bag-holder hand-off from private equity and didn't immediately lose large chunks of its value after the first day.

62275335
>>62273611
The problem isn't losing money; it's tens of millions if not more basically being forced to have a stake in what's going to be a shit show of an IPO. Most people won't even know that this is happening.

There are also a lot of people with target date retirement funds and reallocating their portfolio, while doable, isn't something that they should have to worry about because Nasdaq changed the rules to allow mega-caps to enter the Nasdaq-100 after 15 days following the IPO. The same thing is going to happen with Scam Altman-Bankman-Fried's OpenAI when that goes public.

62276783
>>62273615
By definition these would not be UFOs then since UFO stands for unidentified flight object.

62276797
>>62273487
It's a scam. I read it's IPO. Unaudited financials. Absolute disaster. There are dog coins with better fundementals

62276808
>>62275335
Pension funds won't even touch software at the moment let alone this shitshow. reddit has this deep need to give Elon their money no matter what but as a stock it is hot garbage.

62276827
>>62275195
Mixue (2097.HK) had a three-month long +100% bull run just after its IPO last year, but now it’s down of course

I don’t think Google/Alphabet ever went back to its IPO level once

62277088
>>62276797
>Unaudited financials.
[citation needed]

62277349
>>62276808
Nigga Reddit hates musky

62277355
>>62275195
Facebook

62277449
>>62273501
AGI will literally never be achieved lmao

62278547
>>62273501
Its gonna be a nothing burger then the government says "whoops we need to bail you out, seize the data centers, oh how convienient you have exactly the hardware we need to run a surveillance state that puts china to shame"

62278606
>>62273487
What's the upside when the IPO is at the peak valuation?

62280676
>>62273615
I love EVs, but boy do they need to get their rusting problems under control.

62282746
You know it's a bubble when a rocket company estimates that their rocket operations is only 1% of the value of their business

62285334
>>62278547
this

62285387
>>62273487
I don't have any money to invest.

62285400
>>62280676
Why would they rust more than normal cars?

62285401
>>62278547
Bullish for LINK

62285438
>>62273615
>self driving will be good by then.
Self driving doesn't have an actual audience because the middle class doesn't go to bars or have kids anymore.

item.title: DON'T STACK GOLD GOY!!! ONLY THE GOVERNMENT IS ALLOWED TO OWN GOLD AND TRANSACT WITH IT!! — 7 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
DON'T STACK GOLD GOY!!! ONLY THE GOVERNMENT IS ALLOWED TO OWN GOLD AND TRANSACT WITH IT!!

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:05 UTC

OP

"FBI raided a CIA officers Virginia home on May 18, they found more than 300 one-kilogram gold bars worth over $40 million..they also found 35 luxury watches including dozens of gold rolexes"...Begs the question why is the deep state stacking gold and using it as a primary form of payment/currency while at the same time convincing the goy cattle that its bad for them to have?

Early replies (first 3)

62285260
That guy requested it from the CIA under the guise of needing it for overseas operations. Since he was a senior officer and thought he'd actually get away with it, that does raise questions about how common it is for intel operatives to embezzle gold and cash, especially for amounts less than $40 million

62285318
>>62285260
gold is perfect for government criminals, they can transport it anywhere without trace, im sure that gold was stolen

62285325
>>62285207
>Glownigger got fleeced by glowniggers after trying to fleeced his own organization
Okay and?
I swear goldbugs get dumber by the day

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285366
>>62285260
>that does raise questions about how common it is for intel operatives to embezzle gold and cash, especially for amounts less than $40 million
Common but not in such an absolutely braindead way.
>>62285318
>im sure that gold was stolen
No retard. He bought it will a credit card.

62285410
>>62285260
>>62285318
Wrong. Glowniggers are literally stacking as much gold as they can because outside of the united states, Gold is the preferred method of transacting, Its actually getting more desirable by most of the world as the west ushers in digitized money. The government and the elites will be transacting with REAL money (gold) while the goy cattle (you) will be working for and paying with worthless digital tokens on a screen. kek....also cia officers are known to carry gold rolexes because they are very valuable and easy to transact with in the middle east,europe and asia.

62285583
>>62285260
>requested it from the CIA under the guise of needing it for overseas operations
>hey could you give me 300 gold bars to destabilize the third world with please
I didn’t think it actually worked like that. reads like something out of a saturday morning cartoon.

62285675
>>62285410
Monero makes gold obsolete

item.title: why does this keep happening — 172 replies — 33.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 172 • Updated: 2026-05-28 15:31 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

62282935
>>62263230
>Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?
Too many people are holding the bags from years ago desperately waiting to dump. I call it the bag pressure.
Wikipedia will be listing bag holders as the death cause of Link in a few years

62282960
If your crypto isn't useful for buying drugs you're holding a shitcoin.
Seethe.

62283111
Crypto crashed again today, while the stock market had another green day at ATH.

If Bitcoin hadn't been used to dump on Link at every turn, it would have kept up with the stock market at the very least.

62283386
>>62269753
It’s gonna be a lot less than 10k to make it. It’s probably gonna be 500. There’s only 25,000 people with more than 1k link. I’m sure 90% with 10k over are 4chan. 4chan is an aberration. 5,000 or so mentally retards who found it long before anyone else.

The make it stack right now is probably very similar to what someone finding out about ETH at $10 could reasonably put in. 10k? 20k?

62284391
beautiful thread, can't wait for $1/LINK/day

item.title: What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day? — 53 replies — 12.3/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-05-28 16:18 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62274691
>>62274690
Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.

62274797
>>62274691
>zero iq take
Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass, which means theyre probably the ones going to implement it.

62274806
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass
Irrelevant. CLPR makes CCIP obsolete.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275500
>>62275410
Staking returns aren't proportional to the Link price, they're proportional to usage fees which are measured in USD.

62275555
>>62274690
Sounds great when does this start fellow marine?

62275595
>>62274690
>Stinkies really thought some stinky russian made a magical piggie bank that would give them returns beyond their wildest dreams

62275635
>>62275555
Two weeks

Impressive digits btw

62275645
>>62274690
chainlink staking is economic security and the incentive is to pay $1 per link per day? good! KABOOM

62275660
>>62274690
What do you not understand about crypto being dead, Web3 never happening, and Chainlink’s “use case” being obsolete?

62275685
no I meant the price of chainlink will be equivalent to $1for every day of the week
I'm not talking about rewards or anything else
the price of chainlink will be $7

62275738
>>62275660
... and then you woke up and realized DTCC is going into production with Chainlink later this year.

62275779
>>62275385
you said that in another thread, whered you get this info? post sauce

62275789
>>62275738
Oh wow, two years of insiders trading at low volume before people realize tokenized RWA are stupid because why even do that when there’s no chance of outsized returns and doing fraud which were unironically crypto’s biggest selling points?

62275818
>>62275779
>you said that in another thread
I definitely did not, I only researched this myself today.

This is all public information, Hedera, Cosmos, Avalanche have it all laid out in their documentation.

>>62275789
just look at this incoherent seethebabble

62275885
>>62275385
>it's impossible to implement on other chains
This is not what they are saying, just check the hedera sub, it's looking rather grim for ccip

62275919
>>62275818
So no actual counterargument? All you have to do is show me any evidence that DTCC tokenized assets are generating ANY hype right now.

62275933
>>62274690
Ok. But.... price?? Oh.. and chart???

62275992
>>62275933
going up

62276072
>>62275789
lolwut

62276114
>>62275500
>usage fees
What usage?

62276153
>>62274691
>Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.
/thread

62276166
>>62275885
>This is not what they are saying
It's not what I'm saying either, you just suck at not being dumb.

>>62276114
DTCC later this year, for instance.

62276216
I'd say chainlink has sizeable state in HBAR. Sergey is not dumb he knew the writing was on he wall when no oher DLT could be as fast or cheap as HBAR.

62277103
>>62275479
ive been found out

62277811
>>62276114
>What usage?
chainlink operates a couple bots that trade between themselves and use their smart contracts so it doesn't look bad. think of it as a marketing expense for them

62277818
>stocks are going parabolic and turd worlders are still dealing in the real world with shitcoins that do nothng
lmao

62278888
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

62278920
>>62278888
Imagine how many baby mamas black link marines with 10,000+ link stacks will have.

62282540

>then they laugh at you (you are here)
>then they fight you
>then you lose
>then they lock you up

62282577
>>62274963
>>62274690

Build rewards were a giant nothingburger waste of time and we've seen very few.

62284567

62284672

>Markets activated

item.title: NOT A DRILL — 20 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
NOT A DRILL

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-28 18:40 UTC

OP

YouTube is demonetizing automated channels left and right. Youtube automation was supposed to be a stable source of passive income and now people are losing their empires. Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?

Early replies (first 3)

62277230
>>62277227
Cry me a river, dickface.

62277233
>>62277227
Saar...

62277354
>>62277227
>Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?
They needed people to upload AI slop for that data to be fed into the algorithms to train them to punish that content.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278141
>>62277227
VLC should sue.

62278168
>>62277227
>you can't make money by showing people content they like because you made it with an easy to use tool that produces good content that people like!!! okay???
shorting goog tomorrow

62278201
>>62277227
Content creation is on the chopping block to be automated. People and agents will do it for free. No more payouts to content creators. More profit for GOOG. It’s pretty obvious, really.

62278236
>>62278168
Nobody likes AI slop, AI schizo. Also, not buying your AI bags, you're on your own.

62278254
>>62278236
"Nobody wants a car" said the horse

62278401
>>62278254
I want to watch actual videos of whores and cats, not fake slop created by jeets.

62278404
>>62277227
get a real job

62278414
What about the AI ads?

62278783
>>62278401
AI Cat weapon doorcam videos are better than any real cat video ever made.

62280180
>>62278404
Being an influencer is a real job

62280218
>>62277227
>inauthentic content
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH
Got 'em!

As someone who makes 100% original videos for fun, this makes me happy. I don't make money, I do get a decent number of views. perhaps this will change things.
I ran some youtube theory through a few AI models. AI predicts more wholesome unpolished "real" content will get popular. This try hard, insincerity "Youtuber" "influencer" model is fading.

62280279
Huge news for India

62280285
thank god. I kept getting recommended this climbing channel that would document 100% hallucinated climbing disasters as if they really happened. all of the stories were framed as anti-western morality tales. probably prompted by a resentful jeet.

62280342
>Nooooo now I have to do my own voiceovers on my YouTube videos instead of using AI TTS and nobody is going to listen anymore once they hear my Indian accent nooooooooo

62281915
>>62277227
Good, I'm getting sick of blocking AI and slop channels.

62284388
>>62277227
not my problem, spammer.

62285357
>>62280218
>100% original videos
meanwhile it's just reacting to shit trending on X

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 30 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-28 17:23 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283037
>>62283018
that's a really good point. I just buyed

62283047
now show other market index's like the FSTE 100, is it even above 2008 levels yet????

62283203
>>62283039
ONE nothing wrong with me
TWO

62283258
It's always misleading as fuck
>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
>It's okay though the stuff you buy from this point onwards is cheap and will grow in recovery
>But what about everything I held before?
>Still rekt for years on end
>We cope by calling that "reducing your average buy in"
Not falling for it personally

62283283
>>62283018
Objective truth. Never doom.

62283382
so why should we be immune from a Japan style 30 year stagnation? We had one before 1960. Allowing retards like Trump in power is begging for it.

62283411
>>62283382
Then buy Chinese or Euro stocks, most of which have already been sideways for ~2 decades.

62283417
>>62283382
Japan largely stopped being innovative desu, not America’s case so far

62283444
>>62283411
Many famous Euro stocks are actually down 30 or 40% since 2000 lol (Mercedes-Benz, Shell, Deutsche Bank, Philips, Bouygues, UniCredit, Veolia…)

62283452
>>62283185
So you need to have over 30m now to have what a millionaire had 1900. That's why millionaire was really special back then. So a million now is not even 30k in 1900.

62283465
>>62283444
I'm also amazed at some Chinese ones. Vanke used to be the flagship of Chinese stock market and the most prestigious real estate developer. The ticker number for it was chosen to be 000002, after 000001 being the Shanghai Composite Index

62283472
>>62283452
That distinction still exists, we call them UHNWIs nowadays

62283495
>>62283465
>Vanke
Looks like a crypto after the rug pull, almost as bad as Vivendi and Orange (the absolute juggernauts of the French stock market in 2000, they were supposed to crush the world with all their ambitious tech… 26 years later, still 90% down after the bubble burst…)

62283519
>>62283018
So they're just flat-out admitting that inflation has been 10% every year since 1950?

62283598
>>62283039
Is this inflation adjusted?

62283626
>>62283598
Dividing SPX by the USM2 is almost the same thing as adjusting to inflation.

62283790
>>62283411
How do you know which stock market might be the next Nikkei? It went up, until it didn't, and then took 35 years to recover

62283830
>>62283790
>which stock market might be the next Nikkei?
All of them

62284284
>>62283018
>just wait like 5 years goy, don't sell, it'll return to green eventually

62284502
>>62284284
>the twin towers of anon's portfolio

62284646
just buy and hold for 45 years, that's my motto

62284879
>>62284502
kek and they're right next to "september 11", I swear it was unintentional

62284961
>>62284646
>DUDE JUST WORK AND DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD FOR 45 YEARS

do goyim really?

62285025
>>62283021
>>62284284
>>62284646
whats the alternative?
>inb4 just buy gold

OP

is imploding right now

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62276473
>>62275591

62278457

hyperliquid is closer to a private blockchain. it only has 27 validators.

62278477
>>62276491
This. A major kike stakeholder in a financial scam - never seen before.
In general, I'll never trust a crypto or financial project with a kike or a software project with jeets.

62278491
>>62278477
is Charles a jew? i always assumed he was a goy with all the western shit
>>62277725
i don't know, i sold all my ada in 2021.. but basically charles been scamming the treasury or something for years, makes sense, not surprised.. recently japanese validators or whatever voted to end funding the ""research"" laps funded by cardano.. charles apperently has always gone apeshit and burned every bridge but now it's become exponential bridge burnings and blockings..
ADA is a scam... nothing new.. but finally seeing Charles flame out in real time is kinda neat

62278891
>>62278491

62279915
The cope in this thread is unreal. Cry more, kiddies.

62280492
The narrative haven't really been evolving. Haven't seen your threads for some time and this is the buy signal I honestly was looking and not hoping for. Because the hate your try to project in inconsequential to you.

62280512
I'm not in ADA since 2021.
But now, i am daily scammed by BTC.
Dunno what's worse (financially) desu.

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448
This cope again
Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698
>>62277437
buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

62284265
>>62275591
did they ever get dapps?

62284324
>>62284091
>buy signal

under $20k btc

62284332
>>62284265
i don't think so, who wants to learn haskell

item.title: I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino — 32 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-28 16:32 UTC

OP

I have 15 in total now.. I am gonna hodl them.

Early replies (first 3)

62270209
>>62270028
Swap for XMR and hodl

62270220
>>62270209
Thats my plan but Monero's price is just too high right now. So, I will just wait and see for now. LTC is near its this years low, so it makes sense holding it or even buying more of it.

62271797
aim for 50 at least, this coin is going to power the decentralized AI agent economy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271814
sooooo who is going to tell him?

62272140
>>62270028
I have 800

62272349
no one mentions MWEB which is one solid reason to be bullish on LTC

62272353
>>62272349
qrd?
Also what is the make it and sui stack for this?

62272539
>>62272353
It's a lightweight privacy extension, essentially. It hides the amount and receiver. It doesn't hide the sender though (though there are some tools like Hector Chu's coinjoin that would essentially do this if people used it.) Make it stack on LTC has always been 84. Same as having 21 BTC since it has a maximum supply of 84 million.

As for SUI, be very careful of VC coins people shill. Lots of insiders staking the premine waiting to dump it. I'm not saying don't buy it, just be aware of that. There will come a day when people that got it for free (including people marketing it, which is probably how you heard about it someone marketing it getting paid in the coin and not disclosing they got a shitload of them for free). Not saying they can't pump, but they can dump very hard and never recover. Fun to swing trade though.

62272587
>>62272539
How many LTC or XMR do you have sensei?

62272618
>>62272587
I stopped buying XMR when it went to $300. It could potentially be the most valuable coin, but it has so many liquidity channels cut off I'm happy with where I'm at. You should probably treat the make it stack as 21.

Both the qubic event in XMR and inflation bug in LTC (even though it was caught and didn't do permanent damage) have made me weary on privacy coins. I think you should have them, but BTC should be your core stack if you're new. If I were just entering the market, I'd probably pick up an LTC stack first since it's so cheap right now, then an XMR stack. Not necessarily a make it stack, but just something so you don't FOMO if they pump. Since BTC.d is so high, it'd pick up the alts for maybe a year (just so you don't fomo them later) and then never buy them again and focus on accumulating BTC at any price. I might be wrong and BTC just accelerates higher, but I don't think it'll go so high so fast it'll make much of a difference if you pick up the alts first. 1st generation PoW is pretty ignored right now except BTC, so they are probably safe bets. Of course I'll keep my on stack amounts a secret. Nothing good can come from telling anyone.

62272639
>>62272618
Thanks, I appreciate the advice :)

62272654
>>62272618
do you think xmr could realistically do a huge X from where it is?

62272668
>>62272654
Yeah. BTC ran to 20k in 2017 on retail fomo essentially. There has been a huge increase in the global money supply since then. Honestly, BTC could go to like 700k and it wouldn't surprise me, XMR could go to 4k and it wouldn't surprise me and LTC could go to $800 and it wouldn't surprise me. All this shit is undervalued. Another thing is fiat always dies. All fiat currencies have died historically and the governments of the world are doing the same thing that has killed all fiat currencies historically. It's just a matter of when. In the mean time though, we still have to live in a world where we settle in fiat, so don't invest more than you can afford to lose. Just treat these things as extra savings accounts.

62272838
>>62271797
BULLSHIT

62272845
>>62272539
No one said a fucking thing about sui retarded nigger he asked what a suicide stack was.

62272863
>>62271814
Who cares? Swap vs keep. It’s still a lose-lose bet in all altcoins atp, even Ai agrees lmao

62272971
>>62272845
It specifically says sui you dumb nigger. Not that guys fault you guys speak in nigger babble slang rather than typing it out.

62273235
>>62272863
Yeah Bitcoin is the only way to not loose money, why even have a bank account just keep all your life savings in bitcoin, since it's so stable and basically a guaranteed profite. You go first anon.

62273441
>>62270220
>Monero's price is just too high right now
Sub 300 is gone forever

62273491
>>62272971
you must be over 18 to post on this site

>>62273235
everything’s fake and gay anyway

62273626
>>62273235
Bitcoin is the only crypto club where there’s a long waiting list to get in and you’ll never get turned away because you didn’t follow the dress code that only changed because your ugly ass showed up or even if the club is filled to capacity

62275417
>>62273626
The fuck are you even trying to say?

62275720
>>62272838
enjoy being poor and destitute when the economy is completely overtaken by AI systems and you are relegated to the permanent bottom feeder caste

62276284
>>62275720
LTC WILL NEVER BE CONSIDERED FOR AI YOU INSANE TROON

62278357
>>62276284
sure whatever you say, in 5 years or so your only income stream will come from sucking troon cock anyway

when they'll pay you 0.0002 ltc for each cock sucking session, you'll think back to this thread

62281080
>>62278357
YOU WILL CRY WHEN LTC GOES TO 0.01 LOL LMAO WHAT A CLOWN

62282347
>>62275417
I’m saying you’re a retard with maybe 1/7th knowledge of how bitcoin actually works

62282992
>>62281080
you'll cry after each cock sucking session yourself, regardless of the price of ltc

62283412
LTC CHADS EMERGENCY GATHERING HOW DO WE COPE WITH THE IMMINENT DUMP TO 30$

62283733
>>62283412
I'd buy a bunch at $30

62284587
>>62283733
b b b based i guess
i'll be to mind broken to slurp above 20$

62284798
>>62283412
Doesn't look dumping much.
